     The Numbers Business Report

Risen (2016)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $36,880,033Details
International Box Office $9,364,033
Worldwide Box Office $46,244,066
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $7,934,274
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $4,438,108
Total $12,372,382
Further financial details...

Synopsis

In the year 33AD an agnostic Roman Centurion is charged by Pontius Pilate to locate the now missing body of Jesus of Nazareth. Along the Centurion’s mission, his doubts of such a supernatural occurrence peel away as he encounters the Apostles while piecing together the mysterious events that unfolded after the crucifixion.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$11,801,271 (32.0% of total gross)
Legs:3.13 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:79.8% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$20,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.3 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,915 opening theaters/2,915 max. theaters, 4.5 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $50,992,512

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: February 19th, 2016 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: February 18th, 2016 (Wide) (Aruba)
February 18th, 2016 (Wide) (Curacao)
February 18th, 2016 (Wide) (Iraq)
February 18th, 2016 (Wide) (Lebanon)
February 19th, 2016 (Wide) (Australia)
Video Release: May 17th, 2016 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for Biblical violence including some disturbing images.
(Rating bulletin 2396 (Cert #49692), 10/21/2015)
Running Time: 107 minutes
Plot point: Religious
Social setting: Bible Epic
Subgenre: Faith-Based Drama
Target audience: Faith-Based Film
Time period setting: Ancient Rome
Source:Based on Religious Text
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Columbia Pictures, LD Entertainment, Affirm Films, Liddell Entertainment, Patrick Aiello
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

In the year 33AD an agnostic Roman Centurion is charged by Pontius Pilate to locate the now missing body of Jesus of Nazareth. Along the Centurion’s mission, his doubts of such a supernatural occurrence peel away as he encounters the Apostles while piecing together the mysterious events that unfolded after the crucifixion.
